title: BJRMH Senior Resident Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for Posts
short_info: Babu Jagjivan Ram Memorial Hospital (BJRMH) Senior Resident Notification 2026 has been released on 21 May 2026 on the official website. A total of 7 vacancies have been announced through the BJRMH Senior Resident Recruitment 2026.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 45 years Age Relaxation: As per rules for reserve category candidates.

qualification.education_qualification: Post Graduate Degree (MD/MS/DNB)/Diploma in concerned specialty, MBBS
qualification.details: Senior Resident - Anaesthesia, Paediatrics, Radiology: Post Graduate Degree (MD/MS/DNB)/Diploma in concerned specialty. If PG Degree/Diploma candidates are not available, the candidate who have minimum 02 year experience after graduation (MBBS) from Govt./Govt. Empaneled Hospital may be considered wherein 01 year experience in the concerned specialty. Preference will be given to candidate with 02 year or more than 02 year experience in the concerned specialty. Must not have completed 03+01 years Senior Residency in any recognized institute including regular or Ad-hoc basis.

salary_details.details: Pay Matrix of Level -11 (Rs. 67,700 - 208700) plus other admissible allowances per month under residency scheme.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the Senior Resident posts at BJRMH?
faq.answer: The application process is through a walk-in interview. Candidates need to attend the interview on the specified date, which is 29th May 2026. There is no separate application form submission deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for the Senior Resident positions?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Post Graduate Degree (MD/MS/DNB) or Diploma in the concerned specialty. For those without a PG degree/diploma, a minimum of 2 years of experience after MBBS from a government or government-empanelled hospital is required, with at least 1 year in the concerned specialty.

faq.question: Q3. What is the maximum age limit for the Senior Resident posts?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for all Senior Resident posts is 45 years. Age relaxation will be provided to candidates belonging to reserved categories as per government rules.

faq.question: Q4. How many vacancies are available for Senior Resident posts?
faq.answer: There are a total of 7 vacancies for the Senior Resident posts. These vacancies are distributed across Anaesthesia (3 posts), Paediatrics (2 posts), and Radiology (2 posts).

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the Senior Resident positions?
faq.answer: The selected candidates will receive a monthly salary in the pay scale of Rs. 67,700 to Rs. 2,08,700 per month.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for the BJRMH Senior Resident recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee for the Senior Resident posts. This applies to all categories, including General, OBC, SC, and ST candidates.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for the Senior Resident posts?
faq.answer: The selection process will involve a Walk-in Interview followed by Document Verification. Candidates are advised to be prepared for both stages.

faq.question: Q8. Where is the walk-in interview for BJRMH Senior Resident posts scheduled?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview will be held at the BJRMH office. The exact address and reporting time will be mentioned in the official notification. Candidates should reach the venue with all required documents.

faq.question: Q9. What documents should candidates carry for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must carry original documents and self-attested photocopies, including ID proof, educational certificates, experience certificates (if applicable), and category certificates (if applicable). A duly filled application form may also be required.

faq.question: Q10. What is the job type for these Senior Resident positions?
faq.answer: The Senior Resident positions are on an Ad-hoc basis.
